name: Rinehimer Bus Lines Inc
districts_or_customers: Crestwood School District (Luzerne County, PA) - reinstated after 2019-2020 dispute
recent_news: Lost Crestwood School District contract in 2019 over clearance/audit compliance issues; sued district; in 2020 board voted 7-0 to rehire Rinehimer (3-5 yr contract, ~$2.15M year 1) and Rinehimer dropped suit.
fit_notes: Independent NEPA school bus operator (~47 buses, ~45 drivers) with single anchor contract at Crestwood SD; owner Scott Henry acquired in 2012 so ~14 yrs in. Litigation history is a yellow flag but contract was reinstated. Reasonable Summit tuck-in to densify PA footprint.

name: Driver Bus Lines
recent_news: No public news found. KUSD (Kenosha Unified) uses First Student for yellow bus service and city school routes are handled by Kenosha Transit, so any role for Driver Bus Lines would be very limited.
fit_notes: Listed website 'driverbuslines.com.au' is an Australian domain - likely a data-source error. No verifiable Wisconsin school bus operator footprint found at 1130 61st St, Kenosha; 3 employees and ~$115K revenue suggests micro-operator or shell. Low priority/likely not a real tuck-in target until existence is verified.

name: PKB Transit Inc
districts_or_customers: Likely South Vermillion / Parke County area schools (Montezuma, IN)
recent_news: No M&A or sale news found. ~17 years in business per Buzzfile; ~$237K annual sales.
fit_notes: Very small family-owned western Indiana operator; owner age ~77 is a strong succession signal but revenue (~$237K) and 4 employees suggest this is likely a handful of routes/buses. Too small to be a stand-alone deal; could be a bolt-on if Summit already operates in Parke/Vermillion County, IN.

name: Confer's Transportation
districts_or_customers: Centre County, PA area (likely Bald Eagle Area SD / State College area)
recent_news: No M&A or sale news found. Operates two locations: Port Matilda HQ and State College yard (231 Old Block Rd).
fit_notes: Independent central PA operator since 1992 with two yards (Port Matilda + State College). Small (recorded 3 employees, ~$107K rev likely understates given two locations). Decent geographic fit for Summit PA densification; need to confirm fleet size and Bald Eagle Area / SCASD contract status.

name: Bo-Mar Transportation Inc
owner_or_parent: independent (operates as Maine Bus Company, LLC / dba Maine Bus Rentals)
recent_news: No M&A news. Family-owned mid-coast Maine operator since 1983; facilities in Brunswick, Topsham, and Durham, ME.
fit_notes: Family-owned mid-coast Maine operator (~18 buses) doing school + charter since 1983, 40+ years in. Charter-heavy mix dilutes pure school-bus thesis but provides density in mid-coast ME (Brunswick/Topsham/Durham). Reasonable small tuck-in if Summit wants Maine footprint.
